claims.claim: Cards should use a 4px base unit spacing system to ensure clean scaling on 1.5x DPI devices, since odd increments like 5px scale to non-integer values (7.5px) and produce blurred rendering.
claims.quote: on devices with 1.5x DPI (Dots per inch), the spacing will scale up to 7.5px, which leads to a blurred result

claims.claim: Body text on cards should be at least 16px to maintain readability, with button label text never smaller than the body font size.
claims.quote: Body text should be at least 16px ... Button labels should not be less than the Body font size

claims.claim: Cards require a visually defined focused state with a minimum 3:1 contrast ratio against the background color to meet WCAG 2.1 accessibility requirements.
claims.quote: at least a 3:1 contrast ratio against the background color

claims.claim: Card designs should define a fixed card height with text truncation for overflow content, rather than allowing cards to grow to variable heights, to maintain consistent grid alignment.
claims.quote: Define fixed card heights with text truncation for overflow

claims.claim: Cards must have distinct visual separation from the background — via border or drop shadow — to be perceived as discrete, interactive units rather than flat page content.
claims.quote: Create visual distinction between card and background via borders or shadows
